text: In this video, we described our surgical technique for purely of clamp robotic partial nephrectomy applied to a complex case of CT-to-B renal mass. In the last year, sutureless approach has gaining popularity for its feasibility and safety, together with promising functional outcomes and shorter operative time and length of hostile stay. compared to standard technique with rhinography. According to this, we move toward a sutureless approach, which was combined to a pool of clamp robotic technique, limiting the use of sutures only in case of unintended violation of renal calluses.

The patient is positioned in an extended flank position for the procedure. A transperitoneal five-port access is established with a three-arm robotic configuration. The camera port is placed on the pararectal line at the umbilicus level, while other ports form a U-shape focused on the tumor. Two suction devices with irrigation are used to enhance visualization of the enucleation plane. Hem-o-lok clips are applied to control arterial feeders during enucleation and removed after monopolar coagulation of the resection bed. A needle driver is utilized for precise hemostatic sutures or to repair any renal callus violations.
This slide shows the crucial step of the surgical technique.

The surgeon mobilizes the descending colon to expose the left kidney. The Gerota's fascia is opened, revealing a large renal mass. The right robotic arm is repositioned after kidney mobilization, and the lateral rim of the kidney is prepared. The fourth robotic arm retracts the mass for better visualization. Monopolar coagulation scores two more margins, and the enucleation plane is developed, coagulating arterial feeders with monopolar cooled robotic scissors.
Hemlock clips are applied to control the principal arterial branches, while two sectional vases with irrigation are used simultaneously to enhance visualization of the enucleation plane.

The surgeon inspects the resection bed, addressing minor bleeding with monopolar coagulation and controlling major bleeding with point-specific hemostatic sutures using 2-0 Monocryl. Hemolock clips are removed to prevent migration into the urinary collecting system, and any unintended violations of the renal calluses are repaired with running sutures using 2-0 Monocryl.

The surgeon places the removed renal mass into an endocatch bag. After ensuring hemostasis with monopolar coagulation, the resection bed is covered with a hemostatic agent. The renal capsule is closed with a hemo lock, and a drain is left in place.

In a recent preferences score matching analysis conducted at our center, we compare sutureless approach with renal reflux approach, both in the setting of purely off-cramp robotic partial nephrectomy. Patients receiving sutureless partial nephrectomy had shorter hospital stay, negligible complications, and increased likelihood of achieving trifecta. However, at Kaplan-Meier analysis, significant CKD stage migration-free survival probabilities were comparable between groups. In conclusion, purely off-clamp sutureless robotic partial nephrectomy is safe as feasible in CT-to-B patients. Patients receiving sutureless purely off-clamp robotic partial nephrectomy have negligible complications and minimal incidence of priority renal function decrease. Compared with renal re-court, a sutureless approach significantly increases the probability to achieve the trifecta. longer follow-up is needed to assess potential benefits of this procedure on long-term outcomes.
